key_highlights: Brent crude tops $100 as Iran attacks ships and US-Iran talks stall, keeping Hormuz blocked
Oil holds gains with geopolitical risk elevated; analysts warn of 'ongoing, absolute disaster' over next two months
Apple announces CEO transition: John Ternus to become CEO, Tim Cook to executive chairman
Euro-zone private sector unexpectedly shrinks as services slump; UK inflation jumps to 3.3% on fuel prices
Japan records 5th straight fiscal year of trade deficits as Trump tariffs hit auto exports
Norway's $2.2 trillion wealth fund lost 1.9% led by tech stocks slide
SpaceX secures option to buy AI startup Cursor for $60 billion; Cathie Wood signals big bet ahead of June IPO
GE loses $20B in market cap on earnings; Honeywell declines after revenue miss and cash flow cut
War boosts defense contractors: Aevex (AVEX) soars 24%, Patterson-UTI (PTEN) climbs 8%
Stock futures rise after Trump extends ceasefire with Iran, but markets remain volatile

sectors.trend: Brent crude surged above $100 after Iran attacked three ships, and US crude stocks posted a surprise draw. Oil holds gains as US-Iran talks stall, keeping the Strait of Hormuz blocked. Analysts forecast an 'ongoing, absolute disaster' over the next two months. Lufthansa cut 20,000 flights due to fuel price and supply squeeze. UK inflation jumped to 3.3% driven by fuel prices.
sectors.implications: Energy sector is a clear beneficiary in the short term, but the broader economy faces stagflationary pressures. Airlines, retailers, and consumer goods companies are warning of profit hits. Investors should overweight energy and underweight consumer discretionary and transport.

sectors.trend: Apple announced a CEO transition to John Ternus. SpaceX is betting $60B on AI startup Cursor ahead of its June IPO. Google unveiled two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia. Tesla boosted spending plan to $25B and Elon Musk says Optimus robot will be 'biggest product ever.' However, Norway's wealth fund lost 1.9% led by tech stocks slide, and GE lost $20B in market cap on earnings. Astera Labs and MaxLinear hit record highs on earnings optimism.
sectors.implications: AI remains the dominant long-term theme, but near-term volatility is high. Apple's leadership change is a positive signal of continuity. SpaceX IPO could be a major catalyst. However, tech valuations are stretched and earnings misses are punished severely. Selective buying of AI infrastructure and semiconductor plays is recommended.

sectors.trend: War in Iran is boosting defense contractors. Aevex (AVEX) soared 24% on 'Buy' recommendation. Patterson-UTI (PTEN) climbed 8% as Q1 earnings loom. Under-the-radar defense contractors are building multiyear backlogs. Halliburton profit jumped on international growth. Anglo American advances coal business sale.
sectors.implications: Defense sector is a clear winner from geopolitical tensions. Backlogs are growing, and earnings are likely to beat. Investors should increase exposure to defense primes and niche contractors. Coal and energy services also benefit.

sectors.trend: Keurig Dr Pepper revenue beat estimates buoyed by cold drinks. Best Buy CEO to leave as company seeks growth path. WH Smith suspended dividend warning of Iran war hit. Sainsbury's says Iran war risks hitting profit. Spirit Airlines nears $500M rescue deal. Avis Budget hit all-time high despite 'Sell' recommendation.
sectors.implications: Consumer sector is under pressure from rising fuel costs and inflation. Discretionary spending is shifting to essentials and value. Retailers with strong private labels or cold beverage exposure may outperform. Airlines face headwinds except for those with government support. Avoid high-debt consumer names.

sectors.trend: Blackstone sees 'best year ever' for IPOs as earnings beat. JPMorgan to add Philippine bonds to key EM index in 2027. UniCredit raises Generali stake. UBS AT1 investors get a win. BlackRock sees risk to yen on BOJ miscommunication. Japan's financial watchdog to meet banks on Mythos AI model. Fed chair pick Warsh makes case for smaller Fed holdings.
sectors.implications: Financials are mixed. Investment banks and asset managers benefit from IPO activity and higher rates. Regional banks face deposit competition and credit risk. EM bond inclusion is positive for Philippines. Fed policy normalization could pressure bond markets. Yen volatility remains a risk.

sectors.trend: Roche expects blockbuster cancer pill launch by year end. Amazon launches GLP-1 weight-loss drug program with One Medical. UnitedHealth sets sights on AI, climbs 7%. Amprius (AMPX) stock soars to all-time high ahead of earnings.
sectors.implications: Healthcare is a defensive play with growth catalysts. Roche's cancer pill could be a major revenue driver. Amazon's entry into weight-loss drugs disrupts traditional pharma. AI in healthcare is gaining traction. Investors should overweight large-cap pharma and healthcare IT.

sectors.trend: Trade Desk (TTD) is highly favored by hedge funds. Magnite (MGNI) partners with AMC Global Media. AppLovin vs. Trade Desk: Wall Street favors one AdTech stock. DoubleVerify launches AI verification platform. NFL defended streaming strategy with Trump administration. Trump media company replaces CEO after stock plunge.
sectors.implications: AdTech is benefiting from digital transformation and AI. Trade Desk and Magnite are well-positioned. However, political uncertainty and regulatory scrutiny remain risks. Trump media's turmoil is a cautionary tale. Focus on companies with strong programmatic and AI capabilities.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk: Iran war and oil supply disruption dominate headlines
Inflation resurgence: UK inflation jumps to 3.3% on fuel prices; euro-zone services slump
AI arms race: SpaceX, Google, Tesla, and Apple all making big AI moves
Central bank divergence: BOJ miscommunication risk, Fed chair signals smaller balance sheet
Defense spending boom: Multiyear backlogs for contractors
Consumer strain: Retailers and airlines warn of profit hits from fuel costs
IPO wave: SpaceX, Blackstone see 'best year ever' for IPOs
Trade tensions: Japan's trade deficits from Trump tariffs
Crypto confidence waning: Wall Street losing faith in crypto
Leadership transitions: Apple, Best Buy, Trump media see CEO changes

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Iran war escalation and oil supply disruption
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase allocation to energy stocks, commodities, and inflation-protected securities. Reduce exposure to airlines, consumer discretionary, and import-dependent manufacturers.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Stagflation: rising inflation and slowing growth
risk_assessment.mitigation: Overweight value stocks, real assets, and short-duration bonds. Underweight growth stocks and high-yield credit. Consider TIPS and commodity ETFs.
Diversify within tech: focus on AI infrastructure and profitable companies. Reduce exposure to high-multiple, unprofitable tech. Use options for hedging.
Hedge yen exposure via options or short JPY. Monitor Fed speeches for balance sheet signals. Maintain cash reserves for volatility.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Trade war escalation and Japan trade deficits
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to export-oriented sectors in Japan and Europe. Favor domestic-focused companies in the US. Consider EM diversification.
Reduce crypto exposure. Focus on regulated digital assets and blockchain infrastructure. Avoid speculative tokens.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to energy and oil services stocks
opportunities.rationale: Brent above $100 with supply disruption likely to persist. Halliburton, Patterson-UTI, and Aevex are direct beneficiaries. Oil analysts see further upside.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy defense contractors with multiyear backlogs
opportunities.rationale: War in Iran and global instability are driving sustained defense spending. Under-the-radar contractors are building backlogs that will outlast current strategy.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate AI infrastructure and semiconductor stocks on dips
opportunities.rationale: Google, SpaceX, and Apple are all investing heavily in AI. Astera Labs and MaxLinear hit record highs. AI is a secular trend; pullbacks are buying opportunities.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in healthcare and pharma for defensive growth
opportunities.rationale: Roche's cancer pill launch, Amazon's GLP-1 program, and UnitedHealth's AI push provide growth in a defensive sector. Healthcare is less sensitive to oil shocks.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider IPO participation in SpaceX and Blackstone IPOs
opportunities.rationale: SpaceX IPO in June with Cathie Wood betting big. Blackstone sees 'best year ever' for IPOs. Early access could yield significant returns.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to airlines and consumer discretionary
defensive_moves.rationale: Lufthansa cut 20,000 flights, Spirit Airlines needs rescue, WH Smith suspended dividend. Fuel costs and inflation are squeezing margins.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against yen depreciation and BOJ miscommunication
defensive_moves.rationale: BlackRock sees risk to yen. Japan's trade deficits and BOJ policy uncertainty create downside risk for JPY. Use FX options or short JPY ETFs.
Volatility is elevated due to geopolitical and inflation risks. Cash provides optionality. Short-duration bonds reduce interest rate risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Underweight European equities due to stagflation risk
defensive_moves.rationale: Euro-zone private sector unexpectedly shrinks, services slump, and war impact ripples across the globe. UK inflation jumps. European stocks face headwinds.
Wall Street is losing crypto confidence. Regulatory and reputational risks are rising. Focus on regulated assets.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets will remain highly volatile over the next 1-3 months, driven by Iran war developments, oil prices, and inflation data. Oil above $100 will pressure central banks to maintain hawkish stances, while growth slows. Defensive sectors (energy, defense, healthcare) will outperform. Tech may see further corrections but AI themes will attract dip buyers. The Fed chair's comments on balance sheet reduction add uncertainty. Expect choppy trading with a downward bias for equities overall.
market_outlook.long_term: Over 6-12 months, the outlook is cautiously optimistic if the Iran conflict de-escalates. AI investment cycle, defense spending, and potential peace dividends could drive a recovery. However, structural inflation from energy costs and trade fragmentation may persist. The SpaceX IPO and other large listings could reignite risk appetite. Long-term investors should focus on AI, defense, and healthcare. Diversification across geographies and asset classes is key.
key_catalysts: Oil price trajectory and OPEC+ response
Fed and BOJ policy decisions and communication
Apple CEO transition and product roadmap
US earnings season: Microsoft, Apple, and other tech giants
Trump administration trade policy on Japan and EU
Roche cancer pill FDA approval and launch
watch_list: Brent crude oil price and US crude inventories
US dollar index and yen exchange rate
10-year US Treasury yield and Fed funds futures
AI chip demand indicators (Nvidia, AMD, Google)
Defense contractor earnings and backlog updates
Consumer confidence and retail sales data
Crypto market sentiment and regulatory actions
